turns.content: I have spent the entire morning doing paperwork and I think I might actually lose my mind.
That sounds like a rough morning. What kind of paperwork?

DEFRA stuff. cattle movement records. tb test documentation. herd health reports. every single thing has to be logged and filed and cross referenced with three other forms. I did not become a vet to be an administrator.

That's a common frustration among vets -- the regulatory burden has been increasing steadily and it eats into time that could be spent on actual clinical work. Is this a regular reporting cycle, or has something specific triggered extra paperwork?

theres a new reporting requirement for bovine viral diarrhoea. BVD. came in this year. so on top of everything else theres now a whole separate set of forms for BVD screening results. and the online portal they want you to use barely functions. keeps timing out. had to re-enter the same data three times this morning.

Re-entering the same data three times because the portal crashes is infuriating. Government IT systems seem to be universally terrible regardless of the department. Is the BVD reporting requirement new for Scotland specifically, or is this UK-wide?

scotland has had BVD eradication rules for a while through scotgov but now DEFRA is aligning the english and scottish systems which means more paperwork for everyone and a new portal that was clearly built by someone who has never met a cow. the interface is baffling. theres a dropdown menu for breed type and it doesnt include highland cattle. highland cattle. in scotland.

Not including Highland cattle on a cattle breed dropdown in Scotland is almost impressive in its incompetence. That's the kind of detail that tells you the system was designed by people in an office in London who've never been north of Watford. What do you do -- select 'other' and type it in manually every time?

aye. other. every single time. and then theres a free text field where you type the breed and I guarantee nobody ever reads it. the whole thing is box ticking. nobody at DEFRA is sitting there going oh interesting another highland cow in the highlands. its just data going into a void.

It does feel that way with a lot of regulatory reporting -- the data gets collected because the regulations say it must be, but whether anyone is actually using it to make better decisions is questionable. How much of your week would you say goes to paperwork versus actual veterinary work at this point?

honestly prob a full day a week. sometimes more. used to be half a day and that was annoying enough. its creeping up every year. new requirement here. new form there. and every time they say itll just take a few minutes. a few minutes multiplied by two hundred farms is not a few minutes.

A full day a week is 20% of your working time on admin. That's significant, especially when you're already covering a large area and your time on farm is valuable. Are there practice management systems that can automate any of it -- pull data from your clinical records and pre-populate the DEFRA forms?

theres software that does some of it but it doesnt talk to the new DEFRA portal properly. the formats dont match. so you end up exporting from one system and manually re-entering into the other which defeats the entire purpose. I looked into it last month. the integration is supposed to be coming but theyve been saying that for two years.

The classic 'integration is coming soon' promise that never materializes. That's the worst of both worlds -- you have the software, the portal exists, but they don't connect, so you're doing everything twice. Is there any collective pushback from vets? The BVA or regional vet groups lobbying DEFRA to simplify the process?

the BVA puts out statements about it every so often. reducing administrative burden on rural vets and all that. very strongly worded letters. nothing changes. the farmers complain about their end of it too. half of them cant use the portal either and end up phoning me to do it for them which is absolutely not my job but what are you going to do. theyre seventy years old and theyve been farming since before computers existed.

So you're not just doing your own paperwork, you're doing the farmers' paperwork too because the system is too complicated for them. That's an extra layer of unpaid admin that nobody at DEFRA is accounting for. It sounds like the whole system is designed around what's convenient for the regulator, not for the people actually doing the work on the ground.

thats exactly it. designed from the top down by people who have never set foot on a farm. anyway. rant over. I need to go back and finish the forms before the portal times out again. cheers for listening.
Good luck with the portal. I hope it stays alive long enough for you to get through it in one go this time. And for what it's worth, the rant was entirely justified.
